title: rubia chitralensis
intro: Rubia chitralensis, also known as the Chitral madder, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Chitral region in Pakistan. It is a member of the Rubiaceae family, known for its vibrant red pigment, which is used for its medicinal and dye properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Rubia chitralensis is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including arthritis and skin conditions.
